steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows 'allows researchers' and precedes 'chemical reactions.' The verb 'allow' takes the pattern allow [someone] to [verb], so the blank must be an infinitive.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — to study: Completes the infinitive pattern (allows researchers to study) and expresses what the technique enables. ✓
  • Choice B — will study: A gerund cannot follow 'allow [object]' in standard usage; 'allows researchers studying' is ungrammatical. ✗
  • Choice C — who study: Past participle creates no valid grammatical structure after 'allows researchers.' ✗
  • Choice D — studying: Turns the verb into a noun phrase, breaking the allow-someone-to-act construction and making the sentence structurally awkward. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
